8.reason: Incorrect queue full condition: tail is not modulo 105 but head is, causing mismatch.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Incorrect queue full condition: tail is not modulo 105 but head is, causing mismatch.

9.reason: The queue implementation uses `tail++%105` which does not keep `tail` within bounds; this can cause overwriting and loss of data when more than 105 elements are enqueued. A linear queue without modulo is simpler and safer.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  tail is never reduced modulo 105, causing it to grow indefinitely and making the circular queue ineffective.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  The queue implementation uses `tail++%105` which does not keep `tail` within bounds; this can cause overwriting and loss of data when more than 105 elements are enqueued. A linear queue without modulo is simpler and safer.

17.reason: Since head is modulo 105 and tail is not, the loop may never exit when tail exceeds 105.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Since head is modulo 105 and tail is not, the loop may never exit when tail exceeds 105.

20.reason: head is modulo 105, but tail is not, so head cannot catch up with tail when tail > 104.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  head is modulo 105, but tail is not, so head cannot catch up with tail when tail > 104.

29.reason: Enqueues neighbors without checking `!visited[i]`, causing duplicate enqueues and potentially overwhelming the queue, leading to traversal errors.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.5 , reason:  Enqueues neighbors without checking `!visited[i]`, causing duplicate enqueues and potentially overwhelming the queue, leading to traversal errors.

49.reason: [The input order is wrong: first integer is number of vertices (n), second is number of edges (m), but code reads into m and n reversed]
